groups.desc:
Questions 21-24
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.title: What is the topic of the research?

options.title: A
options.content: the effect of music on consumers

options.title: B
options.content: the effect of music on eating

options.title: C
options.content: the effect of music on the price of food

questions.title: The research finds out in the restaurant

options.title: B
options.content: the music is played at a uniform volume level.

options.title: C
options.content: the music is played at a certain time.

questions.title: The first few questions in the questionnaire are

options.title: A
options.content: to understand people's taste in music.

options.title: C
options.content: to disguise the purpose of the survey.

questions.title: The questions in the questionnaire came from

groups.question_title: Which TWO are the main reasons given for choosing a restaurant?
groups.desc:
Questions 25 and 26
Choose TWO letters, A-E.

groups.question_title: What is people's attitude toward playing each of the following music in restaurants?
groups.desc:
Questions 27-30
What is people's attitude toward playing each of the following music in restaurants?
Choose FOUR answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-F, next to Questions 27-30.

list.title: A
list.content: People will spend more money in the restaurant.

list.title: B
list.content: People don't even notice the music.

list.title: D
list.content: People don't think the restaurant is worth the price.

list.title: E
list.content: People will leave the restaurant right after eating.

list.title: F
list.content: People don't like the restaurant at all.

transcript.speaker: Jeannie
transcript.en: Hi Dan.

transcript.speaker: Jeannie
transcript.en: How's it going?

transcript.speaker: Dan
transcript.en: Hello Jeannie.

transcript.speaker: Dan
transcript.en: Pretty well, thanks.

transcript.speaker: Dan
transcript.en: Have you finished your research yet?

transcript.speaker: Jeannie
transcript.en: Yes, it's all done.

transcript.speaker: Jeannie
transcript.en: I've spent several weeks on it, from the very beginning of deciding the topic, to carrying out the research, and writing the essay.

transcript.speaker: Dan
transcript.en: Congratulations!

transcript.speaker: Dan
transcript.en: It seems like a big project.

transcript.speaker: Dan
transcript.en: I know the research is to do with music, but what is it exactly about?

transcript.speaker: Jeannie
transcript.en: Well, at first I didn't have a specific purpose until one day I read a report that said 'if appropriate music is played while people are eating, it may cause some changes in their behaviour'.

transcript.speaker: Jeannie
transcript.en: But the report didn't illustrate the changes in detail, so I wanted to carry out a thorough investigation into this.

transcript.speaker: Dan
transcript.en: That's quite unique.

transcript.speaker: Dan
transcript.en: So, how did you conduct your research?

transcript.speaker: Jeannie
transcript.en: I started by looking for five different restaurants, and then went to each of the restaurants to order some food and observe other people.

transcript.speaker: Jeannie
transcript.en: I mainly focused on how the music is played instead of how people eat.

transcript.speaker: Jeannie
transcript.en: Findings of my observations indicate that the five restaurants have something in common – they all have a special section to play the music.

transcript.speaker: Jeannie
transcript.en: More importantly, although the music is played at different times, the volume is unchanged.

transcript.speaker: Dan
transcript.en: Sounds attractive.

transcript.speaker: Dan
transcript.en: I've never read anything about this before.

transcript.speaker: Dan
transcript.en: In addition to observation, what other research methods have you used?

transcript.speaker: Jeannie
transcript.en: I also distributed the survey questionnaire to people dining there.

transcript.speaker: Dan
transcript.en: What questions are posed in your questionnaire?

transcript.speaker: Jeannie
transcript.en: Well, the first section of the questionnaire asks subjects to indicate the number of times they have been to the restaurant previously, the number of people they dine with and whether they think they will return to the restaurant.

transcript.speaker: Jeannie
transcript.en: These early questions don't involve musical preferences, but largely act as 'distracters' – the intention being that subjects would be less inclined to think that the survey is about the music being played.

transcript.speaker: Dan
transcript.en: That sounds very reasonable.

transcript.speaker: Dan
transcript.en: Did you design the questionnaire by yourself?

transcript.speaker: Jeannie
transcript.en: Actually no, I read an assignment a student wrote last year, which was just kept beside a dictionary in the library.

transcript.speaker: Jeannie
transcript.en: I came across it when I was looking something up in the dictionary.

transcript.speaker: Dan
transcript.en: Oh, you were so lucky.

transcript.speaker: Dan
transcript.en: Is there any other question included in the questionnaire?

transcript.speaker: Jeannie
transcript.en: Yes, subjects are also asked why they choose to dine at a particular restaurant.

transcript.speaker: Jeannie
transcript.en: Several reasons are listed in the questionnaire.

transcript.speaker: Jeannie
transcript.en: I thought people would put service first, but that's not the case.

transcript.speaker: Jeannie
transcript.en: Only about 12% of them have chosen this reason, while the majority of respondents think whether they will pick this restaurant depends on how many available seats it has.

transcript.speaker: Jeannie
transcript.en: They think it's terrible to eat in a crowded place.

transcript.speaker: Dan
transcript.en: Yes, everyone wants to enjoy a meal comfortably.

transcript.speaker: Dan
transcript.en: Then I guess maybe the location of a restaurant is an important factor since no one likes to waste too much time on the way?

transcript.speaker: Jeannie
transcript.en: It used to be, but nowadays nearly every household has a private car, so they don't have to be worried about the traffic problem.

transcript.speaker: Jeannie
transcript.en: What I find interesting, however, is that a large percentage of people say they will compare different restaurants in the neighbourhood on the Internet first.

transcript.speaker: Jeannie
transcript.en: If one of them stands out from its rivals, they will choose it.

transcript.speaker: Dan
transcript.en: Wow, it's really interesting.

transcript.speaker: Dan
transcript.en: What else then?

transcript.speaker: Jeannie
transcript.en: Well, the second section of the questionnaire asks subjects to respond to the music being played by rating so that the relationship between music and perceived atmosphere could be examined directly.

transcript.speaker: Jeannie
transcript.en: As a means of examining purchase intention, the final section of the questionnaire asks them to indicate the maximum amount of money they are prepared to spend on their main meal.

transcript.speaker: Dan
transcript.en: So what have you finally discovered?

transcript.speaker: Jeannie
transcript.en: I collected all the questionnaires and analysed people's answers to get a clear idea of their reactions to different music conditions.

transcript.speaker: Jeannie
transcript.en: The result shows that music has become an indispensable part of restaurants.

transcript.speaker: Jeannie
transcript.en: If people find there isn't any music during the meal, they will think the restaurant charges too much.

transcript.speaker: Dan
transcript.en: Yes, now music is everywhere in our life.

transcript.speaker: Dan
transcript.en: Is there any difference in people's attitudes towards different musical styles?

transcript.speaker: Jeannie
transcript.en: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Jeannie
transcript.en: Most people think that if jazz is being played when eating, they tend to stay at the restaurant longer and order more food to enjoy the music.

transcript.speaker: Jeannie
transcript.en: But this is quite the opposite of what happens when classical music is played.

transcript.speaker: Jeannie
transcript.en: Many customers think if the restaurant plays classical music, they wouldn't like to stay there for another minute, but to finish the food quickly and hurry away, because they don't like this type of music at all.

transcript.speaker: Jeannie
transcript.en: And lastly, about pop music, it's very popular these days, especially among young people, as I've noticed in restaurants.

transcript.speaker: Jeannie
transcript.en: They like listening to this type of music during their meals.

transcript.speaker: Jeannie
transcript.en: As a result, this greatly increases the possibility of their presence at the same restaurant.

transcript.speaker: Dan
transcript.en: Great.

transcript.speaker: Dan
transcript.en: I've learned a lot from your research.

transcript.speaker: Dan
transcript.en: I think it's meaningful and innovative.

transcript.speaker: Dan
transcript.en: Thanks for telling me, Jeannie.

transcript.speaker: Jeannie
transcript.en: You are welcome, Dan.

21.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 topic。
题目解析：注意题干定位词 topic 容易发生替换，在做题过程中不可死板地抓字眼，而应通过语义的推进进行定位。本题可通过 Dan 的提问“… what is it exactly about?”定位，答案出现在 Jeannie 的回答中：“… I read a report that said 'if appropriate music is played while people are eating, it may cause some changes in their behaviour'. But the report didn't illustrate the changes in detail, so I wanted to carry out a thorough investigation into this.”其中 changes 为选项中 effect 的同义替换。切勿因录音中提到 eating 而错选 B，因为录音中强调的是播放的音乐对消费者行为的影响（music … may cause some changes in their behaviour）。因此答案为 A。

22.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 restaurant。
题目解析：定位词出现得较早，但通过题干可知，本题考查的是研究发现，所以在听到 research finds out 的同义替换 Findings … indicate 时应集中注意力。首先听到：“… they all have a special section to play the music.”其中 a special section 与选项 A 中的 everywhere 矛盾，可排除。随后听到：“More importantly, although the music is played at different times, the volume is unchanged.”其中 at different times 与选项 C 中的 at a certain time 矛盾，故排除选项 C，而 unchanged（不变）替换了选项 B 中的 uniform（一致的）。因此答案为 B。

23.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 first few questions、questionnaire。
题目解析：定位词 questionnaire 原词重现，而另一个定位词 first few questions 在录音中被替换为 the first section 以及之后出现的 These early questions，从而锁定答案句：“These early questions don't involve musical preferences, but largely act as 'distracters' – the intention being that subjects would be less inclined to think that the survey is about the music being played.”此处用了 not … but … 结构，否定了前半句，肯定了后半句。其中前半句中的 musical preferences 对应选项 A 中的 taste in music，可排除；而后半句中的 distracters 以及 be less inclined to think 为选项 C 中 disguise（掩饰）的同义替换，故正确。选项 B 与选项 C 本身为相反表达，也可排除。因此答案为 C。

24.analysis:
听前预测：提示词 came from。
题目解析：本题没有很好的定位词。题目考查问卷问题的来源，注意通过语义的推进定位。通过 Dan 的提问“Did you design the questionnaire by yourself?”可知题干信息的出现，Jeannie 对此予以否定（Actually no），故排除选项 C。接着提到：“I read an assignment a student wrote last year, which was just kept beside a dictionary in the library.”其中 assignment 对应选项 A 中的 study，last year 对应 previous。虽然选项 B 中的 dictionary 出现在录音中，但并非问卷问题的来源。因此答案为 A。

25.analysis:
听前预测：提示词 main reasons。
题目解析：通过 Jeannie 提到的 why 以及 Several reasons 可推断与题干相关的信息即将出现。首先听到选项 D 中的 service，不过随后以 but that's not the case 否定，并在下一句中进行了解释，指出只有 12% 的人选了这一原因，可排除。接着由转折信号词 while 引出第一个答案信息：“… while the majority of respondents think whether they will pick this restaurant depends on how many available seats it has.”其中 how many available seats 替换了选项 E 的 seat capacity，故选项 E 正确。然后听到了选项 C 中 transport 的相关信息，不过 Jeannie 通过 It used to be 及 don't have to be worried 予以否定，可排除。最后由转折信号词 however 引出第二个答案信息：“… a large percentage of people say they will compare different restaurants in the neighbourhood on the Internet first. If one of them stands out from its rivals, they will choose it.”其中 in the neighbourhood 替换了选项 A 中的 nearby，rivals 替换了 competitors，故选项 A 正确。选项 B 在录音中未涉及。因此答案为 A & E。

26.analysis:
听前预测：提示词 main reasons。
题目解析：通过 Jeannie 提到的 why 以及 Several reasons 可推断与题干相关的信息即将出现。首先听到选项 D 中的 service，不过随后以 but that's not the case 否定，并在下一句中进行了解释，指出只有 12% 的人选了这一原因，可排除。接着由转折信号词 while 引出第一个答案信息：“… while the majority of respondents think whether they will pick this restaurant depends on how many available seats it has.”其中 how many available seats 替换了选项 E 的 seat capacity，故选项 E 正确。然后听到了选项 C 中 transport 的相关信息，不过 Jeannie 通过 It used to be 及 don't have to be worried 予以否定，可排除。最后由转折信号词 however 引出第二个答案信息：“… a large percentage of people say they will compare different restaurants in the neighbourhood on the Internet first. If one of them stands out from its rivals, they will choose it.”其中 in the neighbourhood 替换了选项 A 中的 nearby，rivals 替换了 competitors，故选项 A 正确。选项 B 在录音中未涉及。因此答案为 A & E。

27.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 no music。
题目解析：定位词 no music 在录音中被替换为 isn't any music，但比较容易识别。之后听到：“… they will think the restaurant charges too much.”其中 will think … charges too much 替换了选项 D 中的 don't think … is worth the price。因此答案为 D。

28.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 jazz。
题目解析：定位词 jazz 在录音中原词重现，随后出现答案相关信息：“… they tend to stay at the restaurant longer and order more food to enjoy the music.”其中 order more food 替换了选项 A 中的 spend more money。因此答案为 A。

29.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 classical music。
题目解析：定位词 classical music 同样原词重现，答案句随即出现：“… they wouldn't like to stay there for another minute, but to finish the food quickly and hurry away, because …”其中 finish the food quickly and hurry away 替换了选项 E 中的 leave the restaurant right after eating。随后对原因进行了说明，注意录音说的是不喜欢这种音乐，并非选项 F 中的不喜欢这家餐厅，信息不完全对应，可排除选项 F。因此答案为 E。

30.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 pop music。
题目解析：定位词 pop music 原词重现，但出现得较早，可通过表示结果的信号词 As a result 锁定答案句：“… this greatly increases the possibility of their presence at the same restaurant.”其中 their presence（出现）at the same restaurant 替换了选项 C 中的 come back again。因此答案为 C。
